user.title: Connect to signal-cli-rest-api
user.description: Enter the base URL of your signal-cli-rest-api server. Home Assistant will connect to it to send and receive Signal messages.

data_description.api_url: For example http://localhost:8080. Do not include a trailing slash.
data_description.device_name: The name shown on your phone under Linked Devices when linking Home Assistant.

menu.title: Choose a Signal account
menu.description: How would you like to provide the Signal account Home Assistant should send from?

menu_options.link: Link an existing Signal account (scan a QR code) — recommended
menu_options.register: Register a NEW phone number with Signal
menu_options.existing: Use a number already registered on this server

link.title: Link Home Assistant to Signal
link.description: On your phone, open Signal and go to Settings → Linked Devices → Link New Device, then scan the QR code below. After scanning, click Submit and wait while Home Assistant detects the linked account.

link_retry.title: Linking timed out
link_retry.description: Home Assistant did not detect a newly linked account in time. Make sure you scanned the QR code in the Signal app, then click Submit to generate a fresh QR code and try again.

register.title: Register a new number
register.description: Enter the phone number to register with Signal. Signal will send a verification code. If Signal asks for a captcha, paste the token here.

data.use_voice: Receive the code by phone call instead of SMS

data_description.number: International format including the leading +, e.g. +15551234567.

verify.title: Verify {number}
verify.description: Enter the verification code Signal sent to {number}. If this account has a registration lock PIN, enter it as well.

existing.title: Select an account
existing.description: Choose the Signal number already registered on this server that Home Assistant should send from.

progress.linking: Waiting for you to scan the QR code in the Signal app. This dialog will continue automatically once the account is linked.

error.cannot_connect: Failed to connect to the signal-cli-rest-api server. Check the URL and that the server is running.
error.invalid_captcha: Registration was rejected. Signal may require a valid captcha token. Obtain a fresh token and try again.
error.verification_failed: Verification failed. Check the code (and PIN, if set) and try again.

abort.already_configured: This Signal account is already configured.

init.title: Signalbot options
init.description: Manage your recipients and receive settings.

add_recipient.title: Add a recipient
add_recipient.description: Add a contact or chat partner. Provide a phone number, a username, or both.

data_description.prefer: Which address to use when both a phone number and username are set.

edit_recipient_form.title: Edit {name}
edit_recipient_form.description: Update this recipient. Provide a phone number, a username, or both.

data_description.prefer: Which address to use when both a phone number and username are set.

remove_recipient.title: Remove recipients
remove_recipient.description: Select the recipients you want to remove.

settings.title: Receive settings
settings.description: Configure whether Home Assistant polls for incoming Signal messages and how often.

error.recipient_needs_address: Provide at least a phone number or a username.

send_message.name: Send message
send_message.description: Sends a Signal message to one or more recipients.

message.name: Message
message.description: The text body of the message to send.

recipients.name: Recipients
recipients.description: A list of recipients. Each may be a configured recipient name, an E.164 phone number, a username (u:name.1234), or a group id.

attachments.name: Attachments
attachments.description: An optional list of base64-encoded file contents to attach to the message.
